location_vision.content: relocate to where there are more opportunities

key_quote.content: I can trade comfort, certainty, and money as long as I can stay alive enough during those years.

skill_targets.content: AI agents, Python, SQL, light frontend, system design

portfolio_goal.content: agent researcher, public project, and first paid customer proof

job_message.content: If the AI-agent goal becomes real work, who is the first customer?
I will target startups that badly need technical work but do not have it, or companies that communicate with customers a lot. I do not know enough yet to eliminate more precisely.

attack_name: Live Job - Service Reality And Repetition

location_vision.content: relocate to where there are more opportunities

skill_targets.content: AI agents, Python, SQL, light frontend, system design

portfolio_goal.content: agent researcher, public project, and first paid customer proof

job_message.content: If the AI-agent goal becomes real work, who is the first customer?
I will target startups that badly need technical work but do not have it, or companies that communicate with customers a lot. I do not know enough yet to eliminate more precisely.
What kind of problem is worth accepting?
How will you measure the value of that work?
I will mainly look at how repetitive the problem is, then calculate how much time I can save if I take the job, and use that to estimate price value first.

attack_name: Live Job - Pre-Meeting Audit And Delivery Obligation

location_vision.content: relocate to where there are more opportunities

key_quote.content: I can trade comfort, certainty, and money as long as I can stay alive enough during those years.

skill_targets.content: AI agents, Python, SQL, light frontend, system design

portfolio_goal.content: agent researcher, public project, and first paid customer proof

job_message.content: If the AI-agent goal becomes real work, who is the first customer?
I will target startups that badly need technical work but do not have it, or companies that communicate with customers a lot. I do not know enough yet to eliminate more precisely.
What kind of problem is worth accepting?
How will you measure the value of that work?
I will mainly look at how repetitive the problem is, then calculate how much time I can save if I take the job, and use that to estimate price value first.
Before a meeting, what do you need to know so you do not quote blindly?
How many hours per week is this costing you? Explain the SOP specifically. What else do you require so an agent can handle this problem?
Is that first-dive audit strong enough to close price?
I would say it is only a good-enough proxy because every buyer has different psychology. I will prioritize safety and clarify everything with the partner. I need to clarify more before quoting price.
